dhp383:1: Having striven, brahman, cut the stream.
dhp384:2: to the beyond of two things,
dhp384:4: go to their end– he who knows.

dhp385:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp386:1: Sitting silent, dustless, absorbed in jhana,
dhp386:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp387:5: But all day & all night, every day & every night,
dhp387:6: the Awakened One shines in splendor.
dhp388:1: He’s called a brahman for having banished his evil,
dhp388:2: a contemplative for living in consonance,
dhp388:4: one gone forth for having forsaken his own impurities.
dhp389:1: One should not strike a brahman,
dhp389:2: nor should the brahman let loose with his anger.
dhp389:4: More shame on the brahman whose anger’s let loose.

dhp391:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp392:1: The person from whom you would learn the Dhamma
dhp392:2: taught by the Rightly Self-Awakened One:
dhp392:3: you should honor him with respect–
dhp392:4: as a brahman, the flame for a sacrifice.
dhp393:1: Not by matted hair, by clan, or by birth,
dhp393:4: he is a pure one, he, a brahman.
dhp394:1: What’s the use of your matted hair, you dullard?
dhp394:2: What’s the use of your deerskin cloak?
dhp395:3: absorbed in jhana, alone in the forest:
dhp395:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp396:1: I don’t call one a brahman
dhp396:2: for being born of a mother or sprung from a womb.
dhp396:4: if he has anything at all.
dhp396:5: But someone with nothing, who clings to no thing:
dhp396:6: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp397:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp398:3: having thrown off the bar, awakened:
dhp398:4: he’s what I call a brahman.

His army is strength;
his strength, forbearance:
dhp399:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp400:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp401:1: Like water on a lotus leaf,
dhp401:2: a mustard seed on the tip of an awl,
dhp401:3: he doesn’t adhere to sensual pleasures:
dhp401:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp402:1: He discerns right here, for himself, on his own,
dhp402:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp403:2: as to what is the path & what’s not;
dhp403:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp404:3: living with no home, with next to no wants:
dhp404:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp405:3: he neither kills nor gets others to kill:
dhp405:4: he’s what I call a brahman.

dhp407:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp408:1: He would say what’s non-grating, instructive,
dhp408:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp409:1: Here in the world he takes nothing not-given
dhp409:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp410:1: His longing for this & for the next world
dhp410:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp411:1: His attachments, his homes, can’t be found.
dhp411:3: has gained a footing in the Deathless:
dhp411:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp412:1: He has gone beyond attachment here
dhp412:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp413:3: his delights, his becomings, totally gone:
dhp413:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp414:1: He has made his way past this hard-going path
dhp414:3: has crossed over, has gone beyond,
dhp414:4: is free from want, from perplexity, absorbed in jhana,
dhp414:6: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp415:3: his sensual passions, becomings, totally gone:

dhp416:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp417:1: Having left behind the human bond,
dhp417:2: having made his way past the divine,
dhp417:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp418:3: a hero who has conquered all the world, every world:
dhp418:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp419:1: He knows in every way beings’ passing away,
dhp419:4: he’s what I call a brahman.
dhp420:1: He whose course they don’t know
dhp420:4: he’s what I call a brahman.

dhp421:4: he’s what I call a brahman.

dhp423:6: he’s what I call a brahman.
